mappings.rationale: The primary VSI for this section, covering the diversity and classification of animal behaviour, stereotyped responses, fixed action patterns, and instinct.
Covers the nervous system's role in receiving information, processing it in the brain, and initiating behavioural responses.
Addresses hormonal influences on behaviour including the interaction of endocrine and nervous systems in controlling behaviour.
Covers the neural basis of behaviour, including how the nervous system processes sensory information and generates responses.
Directly relevant to photoperiodism and the influence of biological clocks on patterns of behavioural response.
Covers the evolution of behaviour including evidence of genetic determination of behaviour and phyletic patterns.
Addresses the neural basis of awareness and responsiveness, relevant to understanding how organisms process and respond to stimuli.
Covers plant movements including tropic and nastic movements, nutation, and other autonomous movements outlined in this section.
Covers insect behaviour including fixed action patterns, taxes, and the hormonal control of behaviour in insects.
Provides a broad framework for understanding behavioural responses, learning, and the influence of experience on behaviour.
